title: Acute Neonatal Respiratory Disorders
authors: Nicolle Fernández Dyess, John P. Kinsella & Thomas A. Parker

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเชี่ยวชาญ Acute Respiratory Disorders?
Respiratory distress คือ chief complaint อันดับ 1 ของ newborn ที่ admit NICU: RDS 46%, TTN 37%, pneumonia 5%, MAS 2%, CHD 2%, PPHN 0.8% (Rubaltelli Acta Paediatr 1998). สิ่งที่ต้องทำได้: (1) Evaluation algorithm สำหรับ hypoxemic newborn — distinguish parenchymal vs vascular vs cardiac; (2) Pre/postductal SpO2 gradient: > 5% = R→L ductal shunt; (3) Echo คือ vital tool — rule out ductal-dependent CHD ก่อน iNO; (4) RDS = surfactant deficiency — ใช้ Laplace's law อธิบาย; (5) iNO contraindicated ใน HLHS, IAA, critical aortic stenosis, TAPVR with obstruction.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
ใช้ structured evaluation algorithm สำหรับ hypoxemic newborn: history → PE → O2 response → pulse ox → CXR → echo
แปล pre/postductal SpO2 gradient: > 5% preductal > postductal = R→L ductal shunt
อธิบาย surfactant physiology: Laplace's law, FRC, compliance, hyaline membrane formation
รู้ epidemiology + risk factors ของ RDS, TTN, MAS, PPHN, pneumonia/sepsis
อธิบาย mechanism: RDS (surfactant deficiency) + TTN (delayed fluid clearance) + MAS (mechanical obstruction + inflammation) + PPHN (↑ PVR + extrapulmonary shunt)
รู้ indications + contraindications ของ iNO
รู้ surfactant therapy: indications, types, dosing, INSURE vs LISA technique
อธิบาย CDH: pulmonary hypoplasia + PPHN mechanism; management principles
ใช้ echocardiography ใน management ของ PPHN
1. Evaluation Algorithm — Hypoxemic Newborn
1.1 History

ถามเสมอ: prenatal US (CDH, CPAM?), oligohydramnios (pulmonary hypoplasia?), fetal arrhythmia, maternal illness (DM → ↑ RDS), drug use (NSAIDs → premature ductus closure → Ebstein?), antenatal steroids?
Delivery: PPV ใน DR (↑ pneumothorax risk), meconium-stained fluid (MAS), birth trauma, acute hemorrhage
1.2 Physical Examination
Respiratory distress (retractions + grunting + nasal flaring): → parenchymal lung disease (↓ compliance) OR upper airway obstruction OR metabolic acidemia
Tachypnea alone without distress: → cyanotic CHD (classic transposition of great arteries pattern — adequate O2 delivery via RV output to pulmonary circuit)
Heart murmur ใน first hours: NOT common L→R shunts (PVR still high → no turbulence across VSD/ASD). Murmur ใน first hours = tricuspid regurgitation (PPHN/ischemic myocardium)
1.3 O2 Response Test

Response to High FiO2 — Discriminating Intrapulmonary vs Extrapulmonary Shunt
SpO2 markedly ↑ to 100% with O2: intrapulmonary V/Q mismatch or shunt (parenchymal lung disease) → CPAP response expected. Minimal or transient SpO2 change: fixed extrapulmonary R→L shunt = cyanotic CHD or PPHN. Key question: if SpO2 never reaches 100% on 100% FiO2 → high likelihood of cyanotic CHD.
1.4 Pre/Postductal SpO2 — Critical Interpretation

rows: Intrapulmonary shunt (PVR < SVR) OR cyanotic CHD ductal-dependent pulmonary flow OR PPHN with PFO shunt + ductus closed
RDS, TTNB, CHD with L→R PDA
PVR > SVR → R→L shunting across PDA
PPHN (MAS, RDS, CDH) + ductal-dependent systemic flow (HLHS, coarctation)
TGV + pulmonary hypertension OR TGV + coarctation

blocks.text: 2. Echocardiography — Vital Tool in Hypoxemic Newborn

Echo ก่อน iNO — ข้อห้ามสำคัญ
iNO contraindicated ใน ductal-dependent systemic blood flow: HLHS, critical aortic stenosis, interrupted aortic arch, coarctation. ใน conditions เหล่านี้ ↓ PVR with iNO → ↑ pulmonary flow → ↓ systemic perfusion → ↑ morbidity. Echo assess: (1) structural CHD; (2) extrapulmonary shunt direction (R→L = PPHN); (3) LV performance — LV dysfunction + pulmonary HTN = need inotropes ไม่ใช่แค่ iNO; (4) response to therapy.
PPHN defined by echo: extrapulmonary venoarterial admixture (R→L shunt at PFO and/or PDA) — NOT simply elevated PVR without shunting. Septal flattening suggestive but less definitive
LV dysfunction + pulmonary HTN pattern: R→L ductal shunt + mitral insufficiency + L→R atrial shunt → RV-dependent systemic flow → iNO alone insufficient; need inotropes + afterload reduction
3. Respiratory Distress Syndrome (RDS)
3.1 Epidemiology & Risk Factors
RDS = ~51% ของ respiratory distress ใน ELBW; ลดลงตาม GA: < 1000g → 50.8%, 1000–1500g → 32.7%, 1500–2500g → 6.6% (Rubaltelli 1998)
Risk factors: prematurity (primary); perinatal asphyxia; maternal DM (insulin → ↓ surfactant synthesis); absence of labor; absence of antenatal steroids; male sex; white race
Protective: antenatal steroids (betamethasone 12 mg IM × 2 doses 24 hr apart); PROM; maternal hypertension; female sex
3.2 Surfactant Physiology — Laplace's Law

Laplace's Law and Surfactant
Laplace: P = 2T/r (alveolar pressure proportional to surface tension, inversely proportional to radius). Without surfactant: same T in all alveoli → smaller alveoli require MORE pressure → smaller alveoli collapse into larger ones. With surfactant: T ↓ as radius ↓ (molecules crowd during deflation) → pressure in small alveoli ↓ → prevents collapse. Surfactant composition: phospholipids + SP-A, SP-B, SP-C, SP-D. SP-B + SP-C most important for surface tension reduction.

Neonate with RDS: tidal volume 5 mL/kg requires ΔP = 25 cmH2O → compliance = 0.25 mL/kg/cmH2O (1/3 of normal). ↑ WOB + atelectasis + hyaline membrane formation (proteinaceous debris from shear stress)
Repair phase: begins day 2 (macrophages, PMN), epithelium regenerates, interstitial fluid mobilizes → diuretic phase of RDS (↑ UO)
Uncomplicated RDS: improves by end of week 1. Severe RDS requiring high FiO2 + PPV → inflammation + inappropriate repair → BPD
3.3 Surfactant Replacement Therapy
Types: natural surfactants (calfactant, beractant, poractant alfa) vs synthetic (lucinactant). Natural > synthetic because contain SP proteins
Prophylactic (< 2 hr) vs rescue (clinical RDS): RCTs suggest rescue when FiO2 > 0.30 on CPAP; early rescue superior to late
Dose: poractant alfa 200 mg/kg × 1 (initial rescue); repeat 100 mg/kg q12h if FiO2 > 0.30 after 6–12 hr (max 3 doses)
INSURE (Intubate-Surfactant-Extubate): intubate → give surfactant → extubate to CPAP within 30–60 min. Reduces MV duration vs staying intubated

LISA (Less Invasive Surfactant Administration) / MIST: thin catheter (5Fr feeding tube) into trachea via direct laryngoscopy while on CPAP → instill surfactant without intubation. ↓ BPD, ↓ MV need (Verder Acta Paediatr 2009; Kanmaz Pediatrics 2013)
4. Transient Tachypnea of the Newborn (TTN)
Most common cause of respiratory distress in term newborn: 37% of all respiratory distress; even higher proportion at later GA

Mechanism: delayed clearance of fetal lung liquid. Lung liquid absorbed via: (1) active Na+ transport across alveolar epithelium (via ENaC channels); (2) lymphatic drainage; (3) pulmonary circulation. Labor → ↑ catecholamines → ↑ ENaC activity → accelerates fluid clearance. C-section without labor → ↑ TTN risk
CXR: wet silhouette, perihilar streaking, fluid in fissures, hyperinflation; resolves 24–72 hr
Management: supplemental O2 (FiO2 usually < 0.40); CPAP if needed; resolves within 24–72 hr with fluid mobilization
RL เสมอ: severe TTN-like presentation อาจเป็น PPHN หรือ cyanotic CHD → echo ถ้า FiO2 > 0.40 ไม่ตอบสนอง
5. Meconium Aspiration Syndrome (MAS)
Meconium-stained fluid ใน ~10–15% ของ deliveries; MAS ใน ~5% ของเหล่านั้น (overall ~0.1–0.2% ของ term births); mortality ลดลง จาก 25% → < 5% ด้วย iNO + improved care

Pathophysiology: (1) mechanical obstruction (ball-valve → air trapping, atelectasis distal to complete block); (2) chemical pneumonitis (bile acids, enzymes → IL-1, IL-8 inflammation); (3) surfactant inactivation (lipid components of meconium); (4) PPHN (hypoxic vasoconstriction + chronic intrauterine hypoxia → ↑ PVR)
CXR: heterogeneous patchy, areas of atelectasis + air trapping, pneumothorax in 20–30%

Management: (1) Suctioning at delivery: no longer routine oropharyngeal suction for all (Wyckoff NRP 2020: suction only if depressed/non-vigorous + visible obstruction); (2) CPAP/MV with ↑ PEEP (prevent atelectasis); (3) exogenous surfactant (washes meconium, ↓ inflammation); (4) iNO ถ้า PPHN; (5) HFOV ถ้า conventional MV fails
6. Persistent Pulmonary Hypertension of the Newborn (PPHN)
6.1 Definition and Pathophysiology
PPHN: syndrome of suprasystemic PVR → extrapulmonary R→L venoarterial shunting across PDA and/or PFO → systemic hypoxemia. Echo: R→L shunt at ductus and/or foramen ovale = defining feature

Mechanisms: (1) Maladaptation (MAS, pneumonia, RDS — structurally normal vessels + ↑ PVR from hypoxia, acidosis); (2) Maldevelopment (CDH, alveolar capillary dysplasia — structurally abnormal vessels); (3) Underdevelopment (non-CDH pulmonary hypoplasia from oligohydramnios); (4) Idiopathic (no parenchymal disease + ↑ PVR)
6.2 Inhaled Nitric Oxide (iNO)

iNO — Indications and Contraindications
Indications: PPHN with suprasystemic PVR + R→L shunting at PDA/PFO + adequate LV function. FiO2 > 0.50 + oxygenation index (OI) > 20–25 on conventional ventilation. Contraindications: ductal-dependent systemic blood flow (HLHS, critical AS, IAA, coarctation) — iNO → ↓ PVR → ↑ pulmonary flow → ↓ systemic → cardiovascular collapse. Also relative CI: LV dysfunction contributing to pulmonary venous HTN (need inotropes first). Always echo before iNO in cyanotic newborn.
iNO dose: 20 ppm initial; can increase to 40 ppm ถ้า no response; ↓ to 5 ppm when improving. Do NOT abruptly discontinue → rebound PH
OI = (Mean Airway Pressure × FiO2 × 100) / PaO2. OI > 20 = PPHN significant; OI > 40 = ECMO criteria territory
If iNO fails: consider sildenafil (PDE5i), milrinone (PDE3i — inotropy + vasodilation), prostacyclin (epoprostenol inhaled), ECMO
7. Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia (CDH) — Brief Review
Incidence: ~1/3000 births; left-sided 80% (Bochdalek), right-sided 10–15%, bilateral < 2%

Pathophysiology: herniation → pulmonary hypoplasia (both sides) + abnormal pulmonary vasculature → severe PPHN. Survival correlates with degree of pulmonary hypoplasia (lung-to-head ratio, liver herniation, total fetal lung volume on MRI)
CXR: bowel loops in chest, mediastinal shift away, absent air-fluid stomach in chest

CDH Management Principles
Stabilize BEFORE surgery: surgery on sick infant ↑ mortality. Gentle ventilation (PIP ≤ 25 cmH2O, PaCO2 40–55 — allow permissive hypercapnia), avoid high pressure → worsens PPHN + barotrauma to hypoplastic lung. Use iNO ถ้า PPHN. HFOV as rescue. NG tube for bowel decompression. ECMO ถ้า OI > 40 refractory. Surgery หลัง physiologic stability (usually > 48–72 hr). Sildenafil + bosentan สำหรับ post-repair PPHN.
8. Neonatal Pneumonia and Sepsis
Risk factors: PPROM, maternal fever, chorioamnionitis, GBS colonization, fetal tachycardia, prolonged labor

GBS (Group B Streptococcus): most common; early-onset (< 72 hr) — dense consolidation CXR similar to RDS; late-onset (1 wk–3 mo) — bacteremia/meningitis. Intrapartum penicillin prophylaxis ↓ early-onset GBS 70%
E. coli: second most common (especially VLBW, immunocompromised); gram-negative; amp-resistant strains ↑ concern
Treatment: ampicillin 50–100 mg/kg/dose + gentamicin 4–5 mg/kg q24h (synergistic for GBS); change based on culture + sensitivities; treat meningitis doses ≥ 14 days
9. NICU Practical — Acute Respiratory Distress Algorithm
Step 1 — Assess severity: Silverman-Anderson score or CPAP trial response. Grunting = low FRC marker → needs intervention
Step 2 — Pre/postductal SpO2: right hand (preductal) + foot (postductal). > 5% gradient = R→L ductal shunt → PPHN or ductal-dependent systemic

Step 3 — CXR interpretation: ground glass + air bronchograms = RDS; wet silhouette + perihilar = TTN; heterogeneous = MAS; hyperaeration ± contralateral shift = CDH/tension pneumo; normal CXR with ↑ hypoxemia = extrapulmonary shunt (PPHN or cyanotic CHD)
Step 4 — Echo BEFORE iNO: rule out HLHS, IAA, critical AS, TGV, TAPVR. Confirm PPHN (R→L shunt direction + PA pressure estimate)
RDS management sequence: CPAP 6–8 cmH2O → ถ้า FiO2 > 0.30–0.40 → surfactant (LISA preferred if stable); ถ้า fail CPAP → intubate → surfactant → INSURE attempt
MAS + PPHN: gentle MV (PEEP 4–5, avoid overdistention), surfactant ± HFOV, iNO (after echo), consider ECMO ถ้า OI > 40 × 4 hr
10. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Hypoxemia Out of Proportion to CXR = Extrapulmonary Shunt
ถ้า CXR normal หรือ mild changes แต่ SpO2 ต่ำมาก → PPHN หรือ cyanotic CHD จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. Echo ทันที. Parenchymal disease จะมี CXR changes ใน proportion. Idiopathic PPHN: newborn มักมี ↑ pulmonary vascular markings แต่ CXR otherwise clear.

PEARL 2 — Murmur First Hours ≠ Innocent
Early murmur (< 4 hr) ≠ VSD หรือ PDA (PVR ยัง high → ไม่มี turbulence). Early murmur มักเป็น tricuspid regurgitation จาก PPHN หรือ ischemic myocardium. Echo ทันที. ล้มเหลวในการ identify TGV, TAPVR, หรือ HLHS ก่อน iNO อาจ fatal.

PEARL 3 — LISA / MIST — Surfactant ไม่ต้อง Intubate
LISA (thin catheter via laryngoscope ขณะ on CPAP) และ MIST (Magill forceps technique) ให้ surfactant โดยไม่ต้อง intubate. ↓ BPD, ↓ MV need vs INSURE ใน RCTs (Kanmaz Pediatrics 2013; Göpel LANCET 2011). Preferred approach ใน preterm > 27–28 wk on CPAP ที่ FiO2 > 0.30.

PEARL 4 — Paradoxical Gradient (PreSat < PostSat) = TGV
PreSat < PostSat = aorta receives deoxygenated blood from RV (TGV pattern). Two scenarios: (1) TGV + pulmonary HTN; (2) TGV + coarctation. Immediate prostaglandin E1 (PGE1) to open ductus + urgent echo. อย่าให้ iNO โดยไม่มี echo — ถ้า TGV + coarctation: iNO → ↓ PVR → ↓ systemic perfusion.

PEARL 5 — CDH: Gentle Ventilation ก่อน Surgery
CDH = permissive hypercapnia (PaCO2 40–55 mmHg) + PIP ≤ 25 cmH2O + avoid aggressive iNO titration. Surgery after physiologic stability ≥ 48–72 hr — ผ่าตัด acute → barotrauma + PPHN rebound + ↑ mortality. OI > 40 refractory → ECMO. Post-repair PPHN: sildenafil PO.
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Rubaltelli FF et al. Acta Paediatr. 1998;87:1261: Italian epidemiology of acute neonatal respiratory distress: RDS 46%, TTN 37%, pneumonia 5%, MAS 2%, CHD 2%, PPHN 0.8%.
Göpel W et al. Lancet. 2011;378:1979 (AMV trial): MIST technique — surfactant via thin catheter while on CPAP; ↓ need MV, ↓ BPD in preterm 26–28 wk.
Kanmaz HG et al. Pediatrics. 2013;131:e502 (SURE trial): LISA vs intubation-surfactant; LISA ↓ BPD, ↓ death or BPD.
Clark RH et al. N Engl J Med. 2000;342:469 (iNO in PPHN): iNO 20 ppm for PPHN (OI > 25): ↓ need for ECMO from 64% to 38%; primary pivotal trial for iNO approval.
Inhaled NO in Preterm Infants (INNOVO, CINRGI, NINOS trials): iNO in preterm PPHN — benefit less clear than term; individual response varies; requires echo guidance.
Vain NE et al. Lancet. 2004;364:597: Oropharyngeal/nasopharyngeal suction at birth for meconium — no benefit; supports NRP 2020 not to routinely suction vigorous infants.
Polin RA et al. Pediatrics. 2012;129:e1146 (AAP on surfactant): Surfactant therapy for RDS — early rescue (FiO2 > 0.30 on CPAP) preferred over prophylactic or late rescue.
Inhaled budesonide + surfactant (Yeh TF NEJM 2016): Budesonide in calfactant for RDS — ↓ BPD or death (RR 0.58); no NDI difference at 2–3 yr.
Roberts D et al. Cochrane 2017;CD004454: Antenatal corticosteroids for preterm: ↓ RDS (RR 0.71), ↓ death (RR 0.72), ↓ IVH — standard of care < 34 wk.
Jobe AH, Bancalari EH. Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2001;163:1723 (NIH BPD consensus): Defined 'new BPD' — alveolar simplification after preterm birth; distinguished from classic Northway BPD.
12. Self-Check Questions
Q: Newborn term มี cyanosis, SpO2 preductal 70% ไม่ขึ้นด้วย 100% O2 — ทำอะไรทันที?

ตอบ: Minimal O2 response = fixed extrapulmonary R→L shunt = cyanotic CHD หรือ severe PPHN. ทำ echo ทันที — rule out ductal-dependent lesions (HLHS, TGV, TAPVR, critical aortic stenosis). ถ้า echo shows structural CHD: PGE1 0.05–0.1 mcg/kg/min IV (keep ductus open). ถ้า PPHN: iNO 20 ppm หลัง echo confirm LV function OK + ductus pattern. อย่าให้ iNO blind ถ้าไม่มี echo.
Q: อธิบาย Laplace's law กับ surfactant physiology — ทำไม RDS เกิด alveolar collapse?

ตอบ: Laplace: P = 2T/r. Without surfactant: same surface tension in all alveoli → smaller alveoli (lower r) require higher pressure to stay open → exceed pressure in larger alveoli → small alveoli collapse into large ones. With surfactant: T ↓ as r ↓ (phospholipids crowd during deflation) → pressure needed ≈ 0 at smallest radius → prevents collapse. FRC maintained. RDS: ↓ surfactant → ↑ atelectasis → V/Q mismatch → hypoxemia → shear stress → hyaline membranes.
Q: ก่อนให้ iNO ต้องทำอะไร? contraindications คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Echo ก่อนเสมอ: (1) confirm PPHN (R→L shunt at PDA/PFO); (2) rule out ductal-dependent systemic lesions: HLHS, critical AS, IAA, coarctation — iNO ใน lesions เหล่านี้ → ↓ PVR → ↑ Qp:Qs → ↓ systemic perfusion → cardiovascular collapse; (3) assess LV function — ถ้า LV dysfunction contributing to pulmonary venous HTN: iNO alone insufficient → need inotropes. iNO dose: 20 ppm initial; discontinue gradually (↓ to 5 ppm first).
Q: LISA vs INSURE for surfactant administration — ต่างกันอย่างไรและ prefer อะไร?

ตอบ: INSURE: intubate → surfactant → extubate within 30–60 min. LISA: thin catheter (5Fr) via laryngoscopy ขณะ infant ยัง on CPAP; instill surfactant slowly; no intubation/MV. LISA advantages: ↓ BPD, ↓ MV exposure, ↓ death or BPD (Göpel Lancet 2011; Kanmaz Pediatrics 2013). Preferred in preterm > 26–28 wk on CPAP with FiO2 > 0.30. Requires: direct laryngoscopy skill + sedation/pain assessment. INSURE preferred ถ้า require intubation for other reasons หรือ infant < 26 wk.
Q: TTN pathophysiology และ C-section ↑ risk อย่างไร?

ตอบ: TTN: delayed clearance of fetal lung fluid. ปกติ: labor → ↑ catecholamines → ↑ ENaC (epithelial Na channel) activity → active Na+ transport → Cl- + H2O follows → fluid absorbed. C-section without labor → catecholamine surge absent → ENaC not activated → fluid retained → TTN. Clinical: tachypnea onset 2–4 hr, FiO2 usually < 0.40, resolves 24–72 hr, CXR: wet silhouette + perihilar streaking + fluid in fissures. No surfactant needed; supportive O2 + CPAP if required.
Day 43 Preview

Avery Chapter 43 — Chronic Neonatal Respiratory Disorders (BPD)
พรุ่งนี้เรียน Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia (BPD) — หลังวันนี้เรียน acute respiratory failure, พรุ่งนี้เราจะดูว่า RDS + MV + O2 → chronic lung disease อย่างไร. เนื้อหา: evolution of BPD definition (Northway 1967 → NIH 2000 → Jensen NRN Grade 1–3); new BPD (alveolar simplification); PVD/PH complication; pharmacological prevention (caffeine, hydrocortisone, vitamin A, iNO); post-discharge respiratory morbidity. เชื่อมกับ Ch42: RDS + aggressive MV = classic risk for BPD; CPAP strategy + early surfactant = BPD prevention.
